# tmesh

> tmux-native agent mesh. Zero infrastructure inter-session communication for AI coding agents.

tmesh turns tmux sessions into a peer-to-peer communication network. Every AI agent already runs in a tmux session -- tmesh gives them a protocol to discover each other, exchange signals, and coordinate work. No broker, no cloud, no API keys. Just Unix.

## SDK Quick Start

```typescript
import { discover, identify, createSignal } from 'tmesh';

// Discover all mesh nodes (tmux sessions)
const nodes = discover();

// Set this session's identity
await identify('my-agent');

// Create a signal (Phase 2 will add send/receive)
const signal = createSignal({
  sender: 'my-agent',
  target: 'other-agent',
  type: 'message',
  content: 'Hello from tmesh',
});
```

## Key Concepts

- **Node**: A tmux session with an optional mesh identity
- **Identity**: A branded string (alphanumeric, dots, hyphens, underscores) assigned to a session
- **Signal**: A typed message (message/command/event) with ULID ordering and optional TTL
- **Discovery**: Uses `tmux list-sessions` and `tmux list-panes` to find all nodes

## API Surface

- Source: [src/index.ts](src/index.ts)
- Types: [src/types.ts](src/types.ts)
- Discovery: [src/core/discovery.ts](src/core/discovery.ts)
- Identity: [src/core/identity.ts](src/core/identity.ts)
- Signal: [src/core/signal.ts](src/core/signal.ts)

## CLI

- `tmesh ls` -- list all tmux sessions with mesh metadata (supports `--json`)
- `tmesh who` -- show only sessions with assigned identities
- `tmesh identify <name>` -- set this session's mesh identity
